prose:
Method provenance block

Property: molecular dipole moment magnitude (|μ|) and Mulliken partial charges.
Level of theory: GFN2-xTB (semi-empirical tight-binding) as implemented in xtb (program label "xtb"; version not reported by engine). Density-fitting is intrinsic to GFN2-xTB (no Gaussian basis).
Geometry provenance: user-supplied Cartesian coordinates in /Users/evane/Desktop/…/carbon_monoxide.xyz; no further geometry optimization – this was a single-point electrostatics evaluation (GFN2-xTB//input xyz).
Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit solvent; continuum models off).
Charge & multiplicity: neutral singlet (charge 0, multiplicity 1).
Software/backend: xtb via chemkit electrostatics skill (CLI shown below).
Convergence & caveats: SCF self-consistent; integrity check passed (sum of partial charges within 4.4×10⁻¹⁶ e of 0). No warnings were issued by the engine.

Headline result
Dipole moment |μ| = 0.76350522 D
Dipole vector (Debye): [ 0.76350522, −1.48×10⁻¹⁵, 1.46×10⁻¹⁸ ]
Mulliken partial charges (e): O −0.034304897, C +0.034304897 (sum ≈ 0 e).

Reproducibility
CLI invocation: /Users/evane/Desktop/Research/Argonne/chem-skills/mcp_server/chemkit_engine/cli.py electrostatics --method xtb /Users/evane/Desktop/Research/Argonne/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/electrostatics-validation/carbon_monoxide/carbon_monoxide.xyz --out /var/folders/ym/_02kbfyd29z17l376783nlm00000gn/T/tmph_81nym4/live.json
The live stdout/stderr log was streamed during the run to a .out file in the caller’s working directory (same timestamped stem as the .json result). The result JSON is at /var/folders/ym/_02kbfyd29z17l376783nlm00000gn/T/tmph_81nym4/live.json.

No experimental comparison is offered; ask if you would like one located and verified.
